assertions.reason: Settings renders a unified pairing code field with copy action, and first enable stores the returned pairing code immediately for display.
Settings now contains only the enable toggle, unified pairing code field, and machine name input; the old join/validate/interval controls are absent.
Machine name is auto-filled from hostname when blank via context effect and blur fallback.
Background timer is hardcoded to 300000ms and cleaned up on disable/unmount.
Beacon panel renders remote machines and repo status fields including branch, ahead/behind, and last commit info.
Disabling beacon clears interval and resets remote machine state.
First enable performs an immediate sync_beacon_push call, persists the returned pairing code, and shows a generation loading state so the code appears as part of the enable flow.

frictions: Browser mode cannot execute Tauri commands, so user-surface confirmation relied on live rendering of the browser shell plus source review and the passing cargo/typecheck suite for backend-backed enable flow behavior.
